title: NOC 80021 – Managers in horticulture | GTR Immigration
description: Managers in horticulture under NOC 80021 are the specialized leaders driving Canada's nursery and greenhouse sectors. As a TEER 0 (Management) occupation, these professionals oversee the production and marketing of trees, shrubs, flowers, and plants, including high-growth areas like medicinal cannabis and hydroponics. In 2026, as Canada invests heavily in year-round food security and sustainable landscaping, these managers are essential for navigating complex climate control systems and automated greenhouse technologies. For immigration, this management role is a premium candidate for Express Entry and specialized Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category8 – Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupationsTEER Level0 (Management)Major Group80 – Management occupations in natural resources, agriculture and related productionSub-Major Group800 – Management occupations in natural resources, agriculture and related productionMinor Group8002 – Managers in agriculture, horticulture and aquacultureNOC Code80021

duties: Plan, organize, direct, and control the operations of nurseries and greenhouses.
Establish environmental conditions (irrigation, lighting, humidity) required for optimal growth.
Determine the types and quantities of stock to grow based on seasonal market demand.
Supervise, train, and manage staff in planting, feeding, and spraying stock.
Identify and implement integrated pest management (IPM) programs to control insects and diseases.
Develop marketing plans and provide technical advice to customers on plant care and landscaping.
Maintain comprehensive records on stock, finances, and personnel for business sustainability.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of a college program or university degree in horticulture, agriculture, or plant science is usually required.
Experience: Several years of experience as a nursery or greenhouse supervisor is mandatory.
Technical Skills: Proficiency in modern greenhouse climate control systems and automated inventory management software is highly valued.
salary_prospects: Note: Salaries for self-employed operators and Master Growers in the cannabis sector can significantly exceed these median ranges.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that despite some sector consolidation, the complexity of greenhouse operations is increasing, creating a need for professional managers with agronomic expertise. Retirements will account for a significant portion of job openings through 2028, particularly in the traditional nursery and Christmas tree sectors.
labour_market_demand: The demand for horticulture managers is Moderate to Good, particularly in provinces with robust greenhouse infrastructures like Ontario and British Columbia. In 2026, the Hamilton-Niagara region and Lower Mainland BC remain top hiring hubs. With over 59% of workers in this field being self-employed, there is significant opportunity for international entrepreneurs to establish specialized nurseries or greenhouse operations in Canada.
Year-Round Innovation: Manage high-tech, climate-controlled environments that operate through all four seasons.
Diversified Industry: Lead operations in sectors ranging from ornamental floriculture to commercial cannabis and forest seedling production.
Environmental Impact: Drive Canada’s green initiatives by managing urban reforestation and sustainable plant production.
PR Advantage: Management experience in an "In-Demand" agricultural sub-sector provides a competitive edge for provincial nominations.
pathways: Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker): High CRS points for management-level experience.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Priority draws for managers supporting regional agriculture and specialized crops.
OINP Employer Job Offer (Foreign Worker Stream): Targeted at managers filling critical gaps in Ontario's greenhouse sector.
Saskatchewan Agriculture Talent Pathway: A specialized route for those with a job offer in the horticultural or agricultural sectors.
